channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Three sisters from London tragically die in Brighton sea
item.description: Three sisters from the Uxbridge area of London, Jane Adetoro, 36, Christina Walter, 32, and Rebecca Walter, 31, have tragically died in the sea off Brighton beach. The incident occurred near Black Roc...

item.title: Climate Conditions and Rodent Populations Linked to Hantavirus Outbreak in Argentina
item.description: The recent hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship highlights the growing public health risks associated with climate-driven ecological changes. In Argentina and Chile, wetter-than-usual con...

item.title: SDP faction under Gabam expels 13 party members and appoints new presidential candidate
item.description: Di Social Democratic Party (SDP) dey waka gbege as di faction wey dey follow Shehu Gabam don announce say dem don expel 13 important party members, including di party presidential candidate, Adewole A...

item.title: Arusha to Host Cape to Cairo International Marathon 2026
item.description: Tanzania is set to host the Cape to Cairo Arusha International Marathon in 2026, an event that is expected to attract runners and sports enthusiasts from across Africa and beyond. The marathon is not ...

item.title: Ranking of Top 10 Most Successful European Club Football Managers by Major Trophies
item.description: This article from Punch Newspaper highlight the top 10 managers wey don win the most European trophies for club football. At number one na Carlo Ancelotti, the Italian boss wey get five Champions Leag...

item.title: SpaceX lodges plans for potential trillion-dollar IPO
item.description: SpaceX, the space exploration company founded by Elon Musk, has formally lodged its IPO filing, signalling its intention to go public in what could be one of the most valuable market debuts in history...

item.title: Haryana Woman, Survivor of Sexual Assault, Dies by Suicide Amid Blackmail Threats
item.description: In a tragic incident from Nuh district, Haryana, a 20-year-old woman, who had earlier survived a gang rape, reportedly took her own life due to continuous blackmail and mental harassment. According to...

item.title: Thomas Massie Loses Kentucky Primary but Builds Potential Anti-Trump Faction Within GOP
item.description: Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) lost his Republican primary election on May 19, 2026, to Trump-endorsed candidate Ed Gallrein, a former Navy SEAL. The defeat highlights President Donald Trump's strong influ...

item.title: Above-normal temperatures prompt early heat warnings in the Maritimes
item.description: The Maritimes are experiencing unusually warm weather, with temperatures reaching 30°C in Halifax and 32°C in Fredericton, prompting the first heat warnings of the year in Nova Scotia. Residents are b...

item.title: Arsenal Fans Celebrate EPL Win While Honouring Raila Odinga at Kang’o Ka Jaramogi
item.description: A group of Arsenal fans recently visited Kang’o Ka Jaramogi to pay tribute to the late former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, recognising his passion and loyalty as an Arsenal supporter. The visit coinci...

item.title: Tanzania Increases Defence Budget to Strengthen Military and National Security
item.description: The Tanzanian government has raised its defence budget by 17.57 percent, approving Sh4.29 trillion for the 2026/27 financial year, up from Sh3.65 trillion last year. The increase aims to modernize the...

item.title: King Charles shares a light-hearted moment with a seagull in Northern Ireland
item.description: During a public appearance in Northern Ireland, King Charles experienced a humorous moment when a seagull left droppings on his suit jacket. The incident occurred while the monarch was engaging with c...

item.title: Four-year-old in stable condition after accidental fall from Maple Ridge window
item.description: A four-year-old boy in Maple Ridge, British Columbia, is in stable condition following a fall from a second-storey window. The incident occurred on Monday, prompting emergency services to transport th...

item.title: TOWNS Foundation Voices Concern Over Senator Legarda's Recent Political Choices
item.description: The Outstanding Women in the Nation's Service (TOWNS) Foundation has publicly expressed its disappointment with Senator Loren Legarda, a former TOWNS awardee, due to her recent political decisions. Th...

item.title: Véron Mosengo-Omba becomes president of DR Congo football federation
item.description: Véron Mosengo-Omba, former General Secretary of CAF, has been officially elected as the new president of the Democratic Republic of Congo’s football association, FECOFA. This marks a big step in tryin...

item.title: EAC Reports Alarming Environmental Decline in Lake Victoria Basin
item.description: The East African Community (EAC) has released its first-ever report on the state of the Lake Victoria Basin, highlighting serious environmental challenges across the region. The 2025 report covers nin...

item.title: NFHS-6 Factsheets Prepared but Await Health Ministry Approval for Release
item.description: The factsheets from the sixth National Family Health Survey (NFHS-6), which was completed in December 2024, have been ready for over a year but remain unpublished. Preparations for NFHS-6 began in mid...

item.title: Exploring the Posh Grandpa Trend in Contemporary Fashion
item.description: Fashion is embracing a new, whimsical trend known as the 'Posh Grandpa', which offers a nostalgic yet refined approach to dressing. Unlike the often flashy or youth-centric trends, Posh Grandpa style ...

item.title: Stella's Support System: Key Moments with Bubbles and Charlie in The Alibi
item.description: In the ongoing teleserye 'The Alibi,' the character Stella, portrayed amidst intense dramatic situations, often turns to her close allies Bubbles and Charlie for emotional and practical support. This ...

item.title: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. comments on teenage mental health and psychiatric drugs
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. sparked concern during an episode of his government podcast, 'The Secretary Kennedy Podcast,' by making an unsolicited comment about being frequently around teen...
